name: Carne Guisada Con Plantanos (Beef and Plantai
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 lb Boneless chuck, cut into -2-inch chunks
1 large Green bell pepper, seeded and diced
2 cups Drained and chopped canned whole tomatoes or
1 tbsp Spanish paprika (available at gourmet shops)
1/4 tsp Cumin seeds, crushed in a mortar
3 medium Plantains of medium ripeness (yellow with black spots)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large, heavy-bottomed casserole, heat the oil over medium heat until fragrant.
2. Brown the beef on all sides.
3. Reduce the heat to low, add the onions, bell pepper, and garlic, and cook, stirring, until the onions are lightly browned, about 4 to 5 minutes.
4. Add the remaining ingredients, except for the plantains and parsley, cover, and simmer for 1 1/2 hours, stirring frequently. Add more stock if necessary.
5. Cut the tips off the plantains, peel, and slice into 1-inch thick pieces. Add them to the casserole and cook over low heat until the plantains are tender, another 20 to 30 minutes.
6. Transfer the stew to a serving bowl, sprinkle with parsley, and serve hot. Makes 6 to 8 servings.
